title: gastrolobium capitatum
intro: Gastrolobium capitatum, also known as poison or poison flower, is a plant species native to Western Australia. It contains toxic compounds that have been used in traditional medicine for their analgesic and antiseptic properties, including the alkaloids ouabain and ouabagenin. The plant's medicinal properties have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and acting as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Gastrolobium capitatum are typically made by drying the plant's leaves and using them in tea infusions or as a topical application.
